# vldaft

> Varying Location and Dispersion Accelerated Failure Time regression.

## What it is

`vldaft` implements the Anderson (1991) VLDAFT model: both the location
(mu) and the log-dispersion (log sigma) of a log-linear survival model
depend on covariates, and log sigma can additionally depend on the fitted
location through a polynomial coupling. The computational kernel is a
Newton-Raphson maximizer with a Gill-Murray-Wright modified Cholesky
safeguard, implemented in both C and Rust.

## Installation

```r
pak::pak("keaven/vldaft")
```

Requires R >= 3.5 and a Rust toolchain (rustc + cargo); install from
<https://rustup.rs>.

Confidential example data are NOT shipped with this package; this
repository provides the estimator, self-contained tests, and documentation.

## Project layout

- `R/vldaft.R` -- fitting function, formula parsing, backend dispatch
- `R/methods.R` -- S3 methods (print, summary, coef, vcov, logLik, predict)
- `R/family_aft.R` -- gamlss2 family constructors (`AFT_Weibull`, etc.)
- `src/*.c` -- C backend (dataio8.c, loscreg8.c, nrmod.c, matrix.c, vldaft_fit.c)
- `src/rust/src/` -- Rust backend via extendr-api (same algorithms, f64 throughout)
- `src/init.c` -- R routine registration for both backends
- `src/Makevars`, `src/Makevars.win` -- build glue, invokes cargo
- `vignettes/` -- methodology vignettes
- `tests/testthat/` -- self-contained tests (no confidential data)

## Key conventions

- Coefficient prefixes: `gamma:` for scale (dispersion) regression
  coefficients, `eta:` for location (mu) coefficients, `theta1`, `theta2`,
  ... for the polynomial coupling terms.
- The `backend` argument picks `"c"` or `"rust"` via `.Call()`.
- Log-likelihood is returned on the log-time scale (no Jacobian). To
  match `survival::survreg`, subtract `sum(d * log(t))` or call
  `logLik(fit, jacobian = TRUE)`.
- The packed symmetric storage for the Hessian is lower-triangle-by-rows.
- Both backends cap parameters at `MAXCOV = 30` (enforced at the R
  boundary); bump the cap in `src/rj8def.h` and `src/rust/src/lib.rs`
  together.

## Supported distributions

Weibull, logistic, normal, Cauchy, gamma.

## Supported censoring

Right censoring, left censoring, interval censoring, and left truncation
(delayed entry).

## Cure models

- `vldaft(cure = TRUE)` fits Poisson-mixture cure models around the
  supported AFT baselines.
- Standalone cure-model coefficients use the `cure:` prefix for the cure
  probability linear predictor; `gamlss2` families use the `nu` parameter
  block for the same logit-scale cure probability.
- Standalone cure fitting is currently implemented for `backend = "c"`;
  standard non-cure AFT fitting remains available through both C and Rust.
- Cure fits use BFGS with a compiled likelihood/score evaluator and numerical
  Hessians, unlike standard AFT fits, which use Newton-Raphson with analytic
  observed information.
- Cure log-likelihoods are evaluated on the time scale. Compare them with
  standard AFT fits via `logLik(fit, jacobian = TRUE)`.
- Cure likelihood changes should check `R/vldaft.R`, `R/family_aft.R`,
  `src/vldaft_cure_eval.c`, prediction/methods, tests, and docs together.

## Build system

- `src/Makevars` compiles the C objects and runs
  `cargo build --lib --release` to produce `libvldaft.a`, which is then
  linked into the shared object.
- `src/Makevars.win` handles the `x86_64-pc-windows-gnu` target and the
  extra Windows import libraries required by extendr (`ws2_32`, `advapi32`,
  `userenv`, `bcrypt`, `ntdll`).
- Rust sources live in `src/rust/` with their own `Cargo.toml` pinning
  `extendr-api = "0.7"` and `libm = "0.2"`.

## Testing

- Run tests: `devtools::test()` or `testthat::test_local()`.
- Tests here are self-contained and use simulated or publicly available data.
- C and Rust backends agree to machine precision on shared datasets.

## When modifying code

- Any change to the C partials engine (`src/loscreg8.c::ac_tpar8`) should
  be mirrored in `src/rust/src/model.rs::ac_tpar8`.
- Changes to the R interface (argument list, order, semantics) must be
  reflected in both `vldaft_fit` (C) and `wrap__vldaft_fit_rust` (Rust),
  and in the registration in `src/init.c`.
- Rebuild with `R CMD INSTALL .` and run `devtools::test()`.

## Reference

Anderson, K. M. (1991). A nonproportional hazards Weibull accelerated
failure time regression model. *Biometrics*, 47(1), 281-288.

## Links

- pkgdown site: <https://keaven.github.io/vldaft/>
- Source: <https://github.com/keaven/vldaft>
